item.title: Spector NS Dimension HP 5 2023 - Present - Plum Crazy Gloss — $1,812.53
item.description:
$1,812.53 · Excellent · Sam's Guitars

Tentatively listing this amazing extended range Spector in stunning Plum Crazy finish. I've had this as a studio only instrument for a few years and now looking to thin down the guitars. Huge low string clarity from the super long scale length. Versatile tonal options, can sound like a Dingwall but also has it's own sound much thicker in low mids. Do not have a box for this so collection only for the moment.


BODY & CONSTRUCTION

BODY WOOD: Solid Alder Wings

NECK:  Maple

NECK WOOD: 3-Piece Maple; Neck-thru

FRETBOARD: Ebony

NUMBER OF FRETS: 24

INLAYS: Offset Dots; 12th Fret Spector Logo Inlay

NUT: Graphite

NUT WIDTH: 1.81″

SCALE: 34-37"


PICKUPS & ELECTRONICS

CONTROLS: Master Volume, Blend, Bass, Mid, Hi Mid controls

PICKUPS: EMG 45DC

PICKUP CONFIGURATION: 2 x Humbucker

PREAMP SYSTEM: Darkglass Tone Capsule


HARDWARE

BRIDGE: Individual Brass Saddle – Multi-scale

TUNERS: Sealed Die-Cast

FINISH: Plum Crazy Gloss

item.title: Ashdown The Saint Soap — $333.23
item.description:
$333.23 · Mint · absno

. UK Sale Only
An online shop's description:
The Ashdown The Saint Soap is part of Ashdown's superb range of 'Roasted' bass guitars. With it's standout offset body, handwound pickup, roasted maple neck and fingerboard, and affordable price point, it's a sure fire winner from the Ashdown team! 
The body of the Saint Soap features a new, albeit already classic looking offset design. Married to the body is a 34" scale roasted maple neck topped with a roast maple fingerboard, delivering great looks whilst also enhancing the tone.

As its name suggests, the bass comes loaded with a Soap Bar pickup - handwound, all-passive and wired through simple master volume and tone controls (with CTS pots). Tonally, you are treated to a rich, organic bass tone with good amounts of punch.

With its standout looks, range of premium appointments and classic, punchy tone, the Ashdown Saint Soap Roasted bass is superb instrument sure to appeal to many bassists!

  • 4-string bass guitar
  • Offset body design
  • Roasted maple neck and fingerboard
  • 38mm nut
  • 34" scale
  • Hand wound soap bar pickup
  • Volume and tone controls

My view:
It is a great looking bass that sounds good too. The construction is excellent and the workmanship and finish are impressive. The finish is called "Ice Blue", but to my eyes it is more like a light sea foam green.

I cannot really comment much about the sounds and playability, which I found to be great, because "to each their own". I like coffee strong and black, you might like it weaker and with milk. I like adding turmeric, ginger, and black pepper; you might not.

I have hardly used this bass because in my infinite stupidity (GAS) I do not have the space to keep it and a certain someone tells me I have too many basses and does not think it goes well in the living room. (She does not like her coffee strong or black!!)

So..... I will have the bass delivered via ParcelForce 24 Hour service, as they are usually more reliable than other services, and it will be securely packaged.

item.title: Sterling Stingray Ray2 Bass Intro Series 2025? 2026? - Electric Blue — $299.76
item.description:
$299.76 · Excellent · absno

I would say mint is a better description, but it is no longer on the dropdown selection.

It still has the plastic on the scratchplate, neckplate and tuners.

I am selling because it was an impulse buy - I used to have a real Stingray or two - but whereas the Ray2 is an excellent bass, it does not have the sounds of the Stingray. Doh! I think that should have been really obvious. So here is a great bass, hardly used, for a really good price.

From the website:
The Intro Series StingRay bass welcomes players new to the Sterling by Music Man family. At the heart of this bass is a passive humbucking bridge pickup for a familiar yet versatile tone. It also features a narrow 40mm nut width, contoured body, and hard maple neck with satin finish for a comfortable, easy playing experience.

Specifications

  • Model: RAY2
  • Body Wood: Poplar
  • Body Colour: Misty Green (MTG)
  • Bridge: Fixed Bridge
  • Pickguard: White
  • Scale Length: 34" (86.4cm)
  • Neck Radius: 9.5" (24cm)
  • Frets: 21 Frets, Medium
  • Neck Width: 1.57" (40mm) at Nut, 2.44" (62mm) at Last Fret
  • Neck Wood: Hard Maple
  • Fingerboard: Amaranth (Purple Heart)
  • Fret Markers: White Dot Markers
  • Tuning Machines: Open Gear
  • Truss Rod: Dual Action
  • Neck Attachment: 6 Bolt Pattern
  • Controls: 1 Volume, 1 Tone
  • Switching: N/A
  • Pickups: H - 1 Ceramic Humbucker

item.title: Squier by Fender P-BASS Affinity Series Electric Bass — $292.09
item.description:
$292.09 · Very Good · Guitar Buyer

Thank you for viewing our product selection! ☆


----------------------------------------------------------------------


[Product Description]


- Squier by Fender P-BASS Affinity Series


[Features]


We've just received a PJ-type bass from the Squier by Fender Affinity Series.


This is one of the Affinity Series instruments that combines the classic design inherent to Fender's direct brand with exceptional ease of playability.


The striking red finish paired with a black pickguard creates a simple yet commanding aesthetic that photographs beautifully and commands attention on stage.


This model features a Precision Bass-style split pickup at the front and a Jazz Bass-style single pickup at the rear, making it a practical PJ-type configuration.


In addition to the thick, focused low end characteristic of Precision Basses, blending in the rear pickup adds articulate attack and clarity—a versatile instrument for any working bassist.


With excellent balance between thickness, punch, and tonal definition, it handles rock, pop, punk, and vocalist-fronted band arrangements with equal ease.


The 2-volume, 1-tone control configuration allows intuitive tone shaping from the full-bodied punch of the front pickup to the articulate clarity achieved by blending in the rear.


With straightforward controls but broad sonic range, it's ideal whether you're picking up bass for the first time, looking for a practice instrument, seeking a reliable backup bass, or wanting something affordable for casual gigging.


The Squier by Fender Affinity Series is a popular line that makes genuine Fender-style character accessible and approachable, prized for its user-friendly nature and practical design.


The PJ configuration combines the powerful character of Precision Bass with the cutting clarity of Jazz Bass—a highly versatile format for players seeking wide-ranging tonal possibilities from a single instrument.


With classic looks, approachable tone, and practical PJ specifications, this is an excellent value electric bass.


Whether as a first instrument or as an affordable backup bass, this is an instrument ready to work for you immediately.


The instrument comes without a case—just the body—but this makes it more accessible and should appeal to anyone prioritizing practicality in their search for a bass.


[Specifications]


Product Name: Squier by Fender P-BASS Affinity Series

Type: Electric Bass

Color: Red finish

Strings: 4-string

Controls: 2-control configuration

Pickguard: Black

Fretboard: Rosewood

Position Markers: Dot position markers

Headstock Logo: Squier by Fender P BASS

Headstock Marking: Affinity

Country of Manufacture: Crafted in China

Serial Number: CY13020575

Bridge: 4-saddle type

Tuning Machines: Open gear type

Neck Joint: Bolt-on

Neck Plate: Squier by Fender engraved


[Measurements]


Nut Width: approx. 40.5mm

Scale: approx. 868mm

Controls: 2 volume, 1 tone

Weight: approx. 4,065kg

String Gauge: 45-105


[Condition]


Overall in good shape with minor scratches and dings throughout, and slight adhesive residue visible near the bottom.


Neck: Slightly back-bowed (playability is excellent)

Truss Rod: Good clearance

Frets: approx. 70% remaining

String Height at 12th Fret: 4th string approx. 2.5mm, 1st string approx. 2.0mm

Electronics: No issues


[Service History]


- Full cleaning

- Neck adjustment

- Fret polishing

- Fretboard polishing

- String replacement

- String height adjustment


Playing condition is excellent.


[Included Items]


Instrument only. (Please note: no case is included)


[Storage]


We maintain indoor humidity between 45%–55% and store cased instruments with humidity control packs. While there is always some possibility that an instrument's condition may shift during the period between listing and sale due to the nature of these items, we take every precaution to maintain the condition at time of service as strictly as possible.


----------------------------------------------------------------------


-------------------------------Transaction Information---------------------------------------



[Important Notes]

* Please be sure to read the following.


- If you have any questions, please feel free to contact us via message.


- This is a used item. Please carefully check all photos and the description before purchasing.


- For returns, please refer to the notes below and our shop policy.


- We strive to respond promptly, but please understand that replies or shipping may occasionally be delayed.


[Shipping]

* We ship via EMS, UGX, or FedEx. (For addresses within Japan, we ship via Yamato Transport or Sagawa Express.)


* Depending on your country, we may be unable to ship due to package size restrictions.

We would appreciate it if you could message us to confirm before purchasing.


* Please note that Wednesday is our regular day off.

(No shipments can be made on Wednesdays.)


- Although it varies by country, items usually arrive within about 7-10 days after shipment.


[Packaging]

Regardless of whether the item includes an original box, hard case, or soft case, we pack every item carefully and securely to prevent damage.

(Unpacking may take a little time — thank you for your understanding.)

Shipping insurance is included, so please buy with confidence.


[Please Be Sure to Read]

If damage during shipping is suspected, confirmation with the carrier and an insurance claim will be required, so please be sure to keep the packaging materials and the item in the condition they arrived in (see our policy).

The voltage in Japan is 100V, which differs from that of most other countries.

item.title: 2006 Rickenbacker 4003 Blueburst OHSC Rare Blue Burst Bass — $4,199
item.description:
$4,199 · Very Good · Guitar Chimp

For Sale: 2006 Rickenbacker 4003 Blueburst w/ OHSC | Rare Custom Color

Up for sale is a 2006 Rickenbacker 4003 in the exceptionally rare Blueburst finish, complete with its original Rickenbacker hard-shell case.

Blueburst is one of the most desirable and hardest-to-find custom colors Rickenbacker has ever produced. Available for only a short period, it has become one of the most collectible finishes among modern Rickenbacker enthusiasts. The vibrant Blueburst gloss finish gives this bass incredible visual depth while preserving the unmistakable styling, tone, and feel that have made the 4003 one of the most iconic basses ever built.

This is a 100% original example that has clearly been well cared for over the years.

Please note: The original hard-shell case is included; however, the original paperwork is not included.

Condition

Very Good – 8.5/10

  • Light scratches throughout
  • Some small dings from normal use
  • Minor clear coat bubbling around the lower side of the bridge

The finish bubbling is cosmetic only and is a fairly common occurrence on Rickenbacker basses of this era. It does not affect playability or structural integrity.

Please review all photos carefully for the most accurate representation of condition.

The bass is all original with no cracks, breaks, repairs, or structural issues.

The neck is straight, the original frets are in excellent condition with minimal wear, and the bass has been professionally set up by our in-house luthier. It plays cleanly up and down the neck with no buzzing or dead spots.

The original Rickenbacker hard-shell case is included. The case is in very good condition and one latch has been professionally replaced.

Specifications

  • Model: Rickenbacker 4003
  • Year: 2006
  • Serial Number: 06 02204
  • Finish: Blueburst
  • Body: Solid Maple
  • Neck: Maple Neck-Through Construction
  • Fingerboard: Rosewood
  • Radius: 10"
  • Scale Length: 33.25"
  • Frets: 20
  • Inlays: Pearloid Triangles
  • Nut Width: 1.70"
  • Neck Depth: .93" at the 1st Fret
  • Neck Depth: .94" at the 12th Fret
  • Pickups: Original Rickenbacker Hi-Gain Single-Coils
  • Controls: Volume, Volume, Tone, Tone
  • Switching: 3-Way Pickup Selector
  • Outputs: Mono & Ric-O-Sound Stereo
  • Bridge: Original RIC Bridge/Tailpiece
  • Tuners: Schaller Deluxe
  • Weight: 9 lbs 4 oz
  • Case: Original Rickenbacker Hard-Shell Case

Electronics & Features

The 4003 remains one of the most recognizable basses ever produced thanks to its neck-through maple construction, legendary sustain, and unmistakable Rickenbacker tone.

Its pair of Hi-Gain single-coil pickups produce the famous "Rick growl" with tight lows, aggressive mids, and sparkling highs. The Ric-O-Sound stereo output allows each pickup to be routed independently, opening the door to an enormous range of tonal possibilities.

The real highlight, however, is the Blueburst finish. Rarely seen on today's market, it shifts beautifully under different lighting and stands apart from the more common Fireglo, Jetglo, and Mapleglo finishes. It is one of the most collectible modern Rickenbacker colors ever offered.

Tone & Feel

This bass has the unmistakable punch, clarity, and sustain that have defined the Rickenbacker 4003 for decades. Every note rings with exceptional definition, while the maple neck-through construction provides incredible sustain and articulation.

The comfortable medium-profile neck feels substantial without being bulky, making it equally suited for fingerstyle, pick playing, and aggressive rock techniques.

At 9 lbs 4 oz, it has the solid, confidence-inspiring feel players expect from a classic 4003 while remaining well balanced on a strap.

Transparency & Photos

As with all Guitar Chimp listings, detailed photos are provided of the exact instrument. At the end of the photo set, you'll find close-up images taken under direct lighting to highlight any cosmetic wear.

We don't just describe the condition—we show it in detail so there are no surprises.

Many customers tell us our instruments often arrive in better condition than expected.

Why Buy From Guitar Chimp?

With over 18 years of experience, Guitar Chimp is trusted by players and collectors worldwide for high-quality, player-ready instruments.

Every bass is thoroughly inspected and professionally set up by our in-house luthier.

We have safely shipped thousands of guitars and basses around the world. Every instrument is professionally packed, fully insured, and shipped with signature confirmation.

📞 Call/Text

💬 Message anytime—we're always happy to help!

This 2006 Rickenbacker 4003 in Blueburst is a beautiful, all-original example finished in one of the rarest and most collectible colors Rickenbacker has ever offered. Complete with its original hard-shell case, it's an exceptional opportunity for both players and collectors.

🎸 Jerald – Guitar Chimp

Providing The Finest Guitars, Basses & Amps Since 2006

item.title: Carvin / Kiesel LB75 1996 - All Koa Fretless with Hard As Nails Finish — $1,499
item.description:
$1,499 · Good · Bass Place

This is a beautiful Carvin LB75 fretless 5-string bass with a solid Koa body and solid Koa two-piece neck-through construction. Fretless Ebony fingerboard with hard-as-nails finish. It has the classic Carvin built by Kiesel quality, original black hardware, and a gorgeous natural koa look that you just do not see much anymore.

The bass is currently set up with Bartolini J-style pickups wired passive with volume and balance controls. It sounds excellent this way — warm, clear, organic, and much more natural than the original active setup in my opinion. You can easily add a full Bartolini preamp in it if you need active, that what i would do but the passive sound is so sweet, it really needs nothing.

I will also include the original Carvin pickups and active electronics in case the next owner ever wants to return it to stock, though honestly, the Bartolini/passive setup is probably how this bass should stay.

The truss rod is intact, still has a little bit of adjustment left, the neck is straight, and the bass plays very well with low action and lots of mwah. This is a serious USA-made Carvin instrument with beautiful koa, neck-through sustain, and a great fretless voice. I only used flatwounds with this bass, despite the fact that roundwoulds can be used on it because it has hard-as-nails finish.

Specs / details:

Carvin LB75 5-string fretless bass
Solid Koa body
Two-piece Koa neck-through construction
Fretless ebony hard-as-nails fingerboard
Bartolini J-style pickups installed
Passive volume and balance controls
Original Carvin pickups and active electronics included
Original hardware
Natural koa finish
34” scale
USA-made Carvin
Includes hard-case

Condition:

Very good used condition. Please see photos for cosmetic condition.

Includes: Hard Case, original pickups and electronics packed carefully in small boxes

Will come with a very tired set of flat wounds to maintain tension while shipping, but you will want to put your favorite strings on it after you receive it.

item.title: Schecter Stiletto Studio-8 - Honey Satin - 8-String Bass - EMG Active - Excellent — $950
item.description:
$950 · Excellent · Savannah Bass

One of the most practical 8-string basses on the market - paired octave strings give you a natural chorus-like shimmer and fullness that a standard bass can't touch. The Stiletto Studio-8 does this without being unwieldy - thin C neck, neck-through construction, and EMG active electronics make it genuinely playable rather than a novelty.

Honey Satin over mahogany and bubinga wings gives it a warm, organic look that stands out from the typical dark 8-string aesthetic.

Specs:

  • 8-string, 34" scale
  • Neck-through construction
  • Mahogany body / bubinga wings / maple neck
  • Rosewood fingerboard / 16" radius / 24 x-jumbo frets
  • Honey Satin finish
  • EMG 35HZ passive humbuckers / EMG active 3-band EQ
  • Controls - volume, blend, 3-band EQ
  • Schecter Diamond Custom bridge
  • Ernie Ball octave strings (.045/.018, .028/.065, .040/.080, .050/.100)
  • Weight: 8lb 12oz
  • Soft Gator gig bag included

Condition: Excellent to Mint - clean, fully functional, no issues. No flaws to report. Photos are part of the description.

Ships insured and packed properly.

item.title: Schecter Hellcat VI - Ivory Pearl - Bass VI - USA MonsterTone Pickups - 30" Scale - Excellent — $899
item.description:
$899 · Excellent · Savannah Bass

A proper Bass VI done right. The Hellcat VI covers the space between bass and guitar - standard guitar tuning one octave down, 30" scale, and three Schecter USA MonsterTone Stack pickups that give it genuine tonal range across all three positions. Ivory Pearl over swamp ash is a stunning combination that looks as good as it sounds.

At 7lb 12oz this is comfortable to wear all night and the 30" scale makes it genuinely easy to play for anyone coming from either guitar or bass.

Specs:

  • 6-string Bass VI, 30" scale
  • Swamp ash body / maple neck
  • Pau Ferro (Morado) fingerboard / 12" radius
  • Ivory Pearl finish
  • 3x Schecter USA MonsterTone Stack pickups - neck, middle, bridge
  • Passive electronics
  • 10.4mm string spacing
  • 2025
  • Stringjoy Bass VI .024-.090
  • Weight: 7lb 12oz
  • Soft Gator gig bag included

Condition: Excellent to Mint - clean, fully functional, no issues. No flaws to report. Photos are part of the description.

Ships insured and packed properly.

item.title: 【used】Ken Smith / BSR5EG 4.4kg #5EG491304FX【Consignment】【GIB Seoul】 — $11,470.15
item.description:
$11,470.15 · Fair · Bass Shop Geek IN Box [Korea]

Ken Smith is widely recognized as one of the world's premier high-end electric bass manufacturers, based in Pennsylvania, USA.


The brand is renowned for pioneering innovations such as the world's first 6-string bass and taper-core bass strings, earning its reputation as one of the true innovators in modern bass design.


This instrument features the iconic BSR body shape, one of Ken Smith's signature designs.


The BSR was developed with the needs of live-performing bassists in mind and is famously used by players such as Marcus Miller and Melvin Davis.


This particular bass is an Elite model, representing one of the higher-tier specifications in the Ken Smith lineup.


Judging from its construction, the body wings feature a five-piece combination of Maple, Walnut, and Mahogany.


The neck is a five-piece laminated construction centered around quartersawn Maple, paired with a Macassar Ebony fingerboard.


As expected from an Elite model, the body utilizes a premium five-piece construction and is finished in lacquer, reflecting the craftsmanship and materials associated with true high-end instruments.


The pickups remain original, while according to the consignor, the preamp has been upgraded to the newer Ken Smith model.


In addition, the mini switches have been professionally modified to provide 3-way Series / Tap / Parallel switching for each pickup.


At the owner's request, the strings have been left in their original condition and have not been replaced by our shop.


Tonally, the instrument is exceptionally balanced across the entire frequency range, from the Low-B to the G string, with a powerful and authoritative fundamental.


The Low-B string is particularly impressive, delivering remarkable clarity and definition while maintaining excellent presence in a full band mix.


Ken Smith's proprietary pickup and preamp design also provides an exceptionally smooth and musical EQ response, making it easy to dial in professional-quality tones for both live performance and studio recording.


Overall, this instrument is an outstanding example of Ken Smith's craftsmanship and acoustic design philosophy, offering the level of performance expected by professional musicians.


Approximately 80% of the fret life remains.


The truss rod functions properly.


Neck condition: Good.


Body condition: Very few signs of wear are present, and the instrument is in excellent overall condition.


Electronics: Fully functional.


Controls: Master Volume (Push/Pull Active/Passive), Balance, Treble, Mid, Bass, Neck Pickup Series/Tap/Parallel Switch, Bridge Pickup Series/Tap/Parallel Switch


Nut Width: 44.6mm


Bridge String Spacing: 18mm


Scale Length: 34"


Country of Manufacture: USA


The instrument will be professionally set up by GIB's experienced technicians before shipment.


Please note that, as this is a consignment item, shipping charges may differ from our standard rates.


Accessories: Hard Case


Written by: Saga


Inspected by: Yeom


Photos: Hyeon


Includes GIB 3-Month Warranty.

item.title: 【used】Alleva Coppolo / LM-5 5.28kg #B50392C【Consignment】【GIB Seoul】 — $9,817.36
item.description:
$9,817.36 · Fair · Bass Shop Geek IN Box [Korea]

Alleva Coppolo was founded by Jimmy Coppolo, who gained extensive experience working at renowned New York guitar shops such as Rudy's Music.


In its early years, the brand operated out of a workshop in New York before later relocating to California, where it is currently based.


Alleva Coppolo basses are widely recognized as the instruments of choice for many professional bassists, including Paul Turner and Michael League.


The model featured here is the relatively rare LM-5.


This particular instrument was built during the brand's New York era, and many players specifically seek out basses from this early production period for their distinctive feel and character.


The body is made from Ash, while the neck and fingerboard are both Maple, creating the classic 1970s-style wood combination.


The neck features a relatively slim profile, giving it a slightly different feel compared to other examples from the same period.


Even so, the neck remains in good overall condition.


Both the pickups and preamp are original Alleva Coppolo components.


Tonally, this bass delivers a refined, modern interpretation of the traditional Fender-style sound.


Its naturally resonant low B string—one of Alleva Coppolo's most highly praised characteristics—is particularly impressive and is sure to satisfy discerning players.


This is truly an instrument whose appeal is best experienced in person.


We invite you to visit GIB Seoul and experience it for yourself.


At the request of the previous owner, the strings have been left in their original condition upon arrival and have not been replaced by our shop.


Approximately 60% of the fret life remains.


The truss rod functions properly.


Neck condition: There is a slightly more noticeable forward bow on the bass-string side, but it does not significantly affect playability. Overall, the neck remains in good condition.

item.title: 【used】Ken Smith / BSR7 EG Melvin Davis (MD7) 2006 5.71kg #7EG524506WX38【GIB Seoul】 — $11,148.01
item.description:
$11,148.01 · Poor · Bass Shop Geek IN Box [Korea]

Ken Smith is one of the world's premier high-end electric bass manufacturers, based in Pennsylvania, USA.


The company is widely recognized as a pioneer in modern bass design, having developed the world's first 6-string bass and designed taper-core bass strings.


This model features the iconic BSR body shape.


The BSR was designed for performing bassists and is famously used by artists such as Marcus Miller and Melvin Davis.


When it comes to 7-string Ken Smith basses, Melvin Davis immediately comes to mind, and this instrument is his signature model.


As an EG model, it represents one of the higher-grade specifications in the lineup. The body features a five-piece construction of Figured Walnut, Mahogany, and Tiger Maple, and appears to be finished in lacquer.


The neck is a five-piece laminated construction centered around quartersawn Maple.


This laminated neck design minimizes variations between individual pieces of wood while providing exceptional rigidity and long-term stability.


The fingerboard is made from Macassar Ebony.


It is equipped with the original dual-coil pickups, each of which can be switched independently between series and parallel wiring.


The preamp is also the original Ken Smith unit.


Thanks to Ken Smith's distinctive pickup and preamp design, the EQ responds smoothly and naturally, making it exceptionally easy to dial in usable tones for both live performances and studio recording.


This bass is a superb fusion of Ken Smith's renowned craftsmanship and acoustic design philosophy, delivering professional-grade performance in every respect.


If you're looking for one of the finest electric basses available, this is an outstanding opportunity.


**SPECS**


Body Top/Back: Figured Walnut

Body Core: Figured Maple with Mahogany

Body Construction: Multi-Laminate Elite Style

Neck: 5-Pc. Maple & Walnut (Neck-Thru)

Fingerboard: Macassar Ebony with Dot Inlays

Finish: Hand Rubbed High Gloss Catalyzed Lacquer over Stradivari Gold Oil Base

Pickups: Ken Smith Original Custom Soapbar

Preamp: Ken Smith Original 3-Band EQ

Hardware: Gold

Serial No: 7EG524506WX・38

Date: 4/18/06 (Quality Control Tested)

Accessories: Original Hard Case


Fret wear: Approximately 80% remaining.


Truss rod: Functions properly.


Neck condition: Good.


Body condition: Various small dents and surface scratches are present.


Electronics: Fully functional.


Repairs/Modifications: None.


Controls: Master Volume (Push/Pull Active/Passive), Balance, Bass, Middle, Treble, Neck Pickup Wiring Switch (Series/Parallel), Bridge Pickup Wiring Switch (Series/Parallel)


Nut Width: 61.7mm


String Spacing: 18.0mm


Scale Length: 34"


Year: 2006


Country of Manufacture: USA

Original rare early musicman Stingray from the pre Ernie Ball Era.This bass is in remarkable condition and the Tone and sustain of this bass is out of this world.The grain on the ash body is gorgeous and the finish is sublime.The weight is really good as these pre EB usually weight around 5kg but this particular one sits at 4.3kg which makes a lot more comfortable to play for long gigs.Being an early one , it has the original high mass bridge with string through the body and raising mutes mechanism.Preamp , pickup and pots are perfectly working with no buzz or scratchy noise.These are skyrocketing everywhere and they are considered the precbs of musicman. item.title: Fender American Vintage II '60 Precision Bass 2022 - Present - Daphne Blue — $1,450
item.description:
$1,450 · Very Good · Closet Candy Bass

American Vintage II Precision in great shape and ready to play. Sets up great, neck is straight, truss rod works as advertised, sounds fantastic. Now at arms length it looks great too. But it has a few minor dings - none are eye-sores or noticeable until you go looking for them - see the pics. The previous owner said he was taking the neck off to do truss rod adjustments and the nitro finish is unforgiving. Now true nitro finishes are easy to chip but I've adjusted this truss rod and I don't take off the neck. I do take off the pickguard and use a thin screwdriver. It turned easily and far less risk of chipping paint. Regardless, it's a great bass and priced to reflect the slight imperfections. Heck, I thought it played great and never notice em myself.

Sets up nice, truss rod works fine, wearing a set of Ti flats right now. Sounds fantastic. Comes with the factory case and case candy. This one is a very clean and sweet looking bass.

🎸 Headless Ergonomic Design

The headless bass guitar design reduces overall weight and improves balance, making it more comfortable for long playing sessions while offering a modern and compact appearance.

Designed with multiscale fanned frets, this 5-string bass provides improved string tension and better intonation across the fretboard, delivering clearer low notes and enhanced playability.

item.description:
$3,694.95 · Good · Ikebe Music

Our Business Hours is Mon to Fri / 11:00 to 18:00 JPN time
We will close Sat and Sun, so we will reply to inquiry that received weekend on next week.
Your understanding will be appreciated.

We are receiving the customer order with first comes first serves basis both on website and at real shop.
So occasionally it will be sold out while having email communication.
Please understand in advance.

Japanese consumption tax is not required for international shopping. However you may be required to pay duty, tax and import fee when receiving your items in your country. You must pay all these duty, tax and fee as receiver/importer and we do not cover any of them.

■Please note
The product description is an automatic translation of the Japanese description. Please contact us if you have any questions.

-----------------------------------

Please be noted this one is USED not new.
There is not any issue on the function and the playing condition but blemishes on the cosmetic.
Please send message for more detail information of the condition.

-----------------------------------

The Fender Precision Bass, the company's first solid-body electric bass guitar, was born and released in 1951. Following the success of the Precision Bass, the Jazz Bass was released later, and both continue to influence the music scene today.

This particular guitar, with its headstock serial number, was shipped around 1978. Its deep walnut color is particularly appealing.

【body】
The overall weight is approximately 4.25 kg. The body is made of ash. The relatively dark, deep walnut color beautifully showcases the ash wood grain in a subtle way. The body finish from this period is "Fender Thick-Skin High-Gloss," which uses polyester as a base coat. There are some areas of rubber discoloration on the top coat, a few dents, belt buckle marks on the back of the body, and some tarnishing on the metal parts, but it can be said to be in relatively good condition. The pickguard has been replaced with a black-white-black 3-ply of the same specifications. The strap pins have been replaced with Dunlop-style ones, and the control knobs have also been replaced. The bridge saddle for the first string has been replaced with a spiral type.

【neck】
The neck is made of maple, and the fingerboard is made of rosewood. One of its charms is the wood grain that shimmers depending on the angle on the headstock and neck. There is some sun fading on the headstock, a few dents on the back of the neck, and some tarnishing on the metal parts, but like the body, it is in relatively good condition. The tuners have been replaced with Schaller tuners of the same specifications. There is not much variation in the winding, and they function properly. The frets and nut have been replaced by our shop and leveled using a PLEK machine, resulting in excellent playability. The headstock thickness is 15.7mm. The nut width is 42.3mm. It has a relatively solid neck shape.

[Electrical System]
The controls are the standard master volume/master tone. The pickups are gray bobbins with clearly visible dating. The pickups and control components are in their original condition.

It boasts a bright attack and impressive tight bass and midrange. When picking, it delivers a powerful, impactful sound reminiscent of the 1970s, while fingerpicking produces a thick, rich bass. Its responsiveness to picking is excellent, ensuring it stands out even in a band ensemble.

Accessories: Fender current model black Tolex hard case

Weight: 4.25kg
Body Material: White Ash 2 pieces
Neck Material/Scale Length: Maple 1 piece/864.0mm (34inch)
Fingerboard/Fret Marker/Radius: Rosewood round laminate/Pearloid dot/184.15mm (7.25inch)
Nut Width/Fret Size: 42.3mm (replaced)/2.4mm (replaced), 20 Fret
Neck Thickness (1F/7F/12F): 21.6mm/23.6mm/24.7mm
Capacitor: Dielectron CircleD 100V
Pickup: Fender Precision Bass Split Pickup Gray Bobbin (10.8kΩ)
*The DC resistance value of the pickup is measured after passing through the control section.

Heartman Vintage Guitars is a specialty store for vintage guitars and basses operated by Ikebe Musical Instruments.

item.title: Bacchus BJB-1R Jazz Bass Type【Current situation merchandise】 — $162.27
item.description:
$162.27 · Very Good · Guitar Buyer

[DESCRIPTION]

Thank you so much for viewing this item among our many listings☆


* Please note: this is an as-is item. We ask that you read the full description carefully and only bid if you are satisfied with the as-is condition.

(We kindly request bids only from those who fully accept the as-is condition of this item.)


----------------------------------------------------------------------


[Product Description]


Bacchus BJB-1R Jazz Bass Type - As-Is Item


[Features]


From the Bacchus Universe Series, the easy-to-handle BJB-1R with classic looks has arrived in stock. The smart shape characteristic of jazz bass models combined with a striking 3-tone sunburst finish makes this a bass that commands presence both on stage and at home.


Equipped with J-type pickups in both front and rear positions, this model offers versatile sonic character ranging from thick, punchy lows to articulate, cutting highs. The straightforward control layout makes intuitive tone shaping easy, and the instrument is particularly beginner-friendly for those just starting out on bass.


The settled character typical of rosewood fretboard construction, combined with the smooth playability inherent to jazz bass design, makes this suitable across a wide range of genres—rock, pop, funk, blues, and more. Finger-style playing yields warm, rounded tones, while pick playing delivers clear attack and definition. This is a well-balanced instrument.


The high value-for-money that Bacchus is known for is a major appeal—at an accessible price point, it offers excellent balance in appearance, sound, and practicality. Highly recommended as a secondary bass or practice instrument. For those wanting to enjoy a classic jazz bass style casually, this is a particularly worthwhile option.


Due to a missing screw on the 4-string tuning peg, this is offered as an as-is item; however, tuning functions without issue. For those unconcerned with cosmetic wear, this is a fully playable instrument offering excellent value.


No case is included—only the instrument itself—which helps keep the price accessible. If you've been searching for a jazz bass type, or if you're looking for an easy-to-play bass at a reasonable price, please don't miss this opportunity.


[Specifications]


- Model Name: Bacchus BJB-1R

- Series: Bacchus Universe Series

- Type: Jazz Bass Type

- Body: Hardwood

- Neck: Maple

- Fretboard: Rosewood

- Scale: 34 inch

- Nut Width: 38.0 mm

- Pickups: Original J Single Type

- Pickup Configuration: 2 J-type single-coil pickups

- Controls: 2 Volume, 1 Tone

- Bridge: Vintage Fixed Type

- Tuning Pegs: Open Gear Type

- Finish: Polyurethane


[Measurements]


- Nut (width): approx. 38 mm

- Scale: approx. 870 mm

- Controls: 2 volume, 1 tone

- Weight: approx. 4,285 kg


[Condition]


There are minor scratches and dents throughout, and a missing screw on the 4-string tuning peg (tuning is unaffected), but overall the instrument is in good condition.


- Neck: Slight waviness (playability is excellent)

- Truss Rod: Adequate clearance in both directions

- Frets: approx. 80% remaining

- String Height at 12th Fret: 4-string approx. 2.5 mm, 1-string approx. 2.0 mm

- Electronics: No issues


[Adjustments Performed]


- Full cleaning

- Neck adjustment

- Fret polishing (dry cloth)

- Fretboard polishing (dry cloth)

- String height adjustment


Playing condition is excellent.


[As-Is Details]


The missing 4-string tuning peg screw is the reason for as-is classification. Since tuning functions without issue, this instrument offers excellent value for those unbothered by this cosmetic matter.


[Included Items]


Instrument only. (No case included—please note this in advance.)


[Storage]


We maintain indoor humidity between 45%–55%, and guitars with cases are stored with humidity control packets. While there is a possibility the item's condition may change if time passes between listing and purchase, we take strict precautions to preserve the condition as it was at maintenance.


-------------------------------Transaction Information---------------------------------------



[Important Notes]

* Please be sure to read the following.


- If you have any questions, please feel free to contact us via message.


- This is a used item. Please carefully check all photos and the description before purchasing.


- For returns, please refer to the notes below and our shop policy.


- We strive to respond promptly, but please understand that replies or shipping may occasionally be delayed.


[Shipping]

* We ship via EMS, UGX, or FedEx. (For addresses within Japan, we ship via Yamato Transport or Sagawa Express.)


* Depending on your country, we may be unable to ship due to package size restrictions.

We would appreciate it if you could message us to confirm before purchasing.


* Please note that Wednesday is our regular day off.

(No shipments can be made on Wednesdays.)


- Although it varies by country, items usually arrive within about 7-10 days after shipment.


[Packaging]

Regardless of whether the item includes an original box, hard case, or soft case, we pack every item carefully and securely to prevent damage.

(Unpacking may take a little time — thank you for your understanding.)

Shipping insurance is included, so please buy with confidence.


[Please Be Sure to Read]

* As this is a used item, only one is in stock.

* Please note that there may be scratches, dents, or other signs of use that are difficult to see in the photos.

* This item is listed on multiple marketplaces, so there may be a time lag in sales and stock updates.


Due to the above, the item may already be sold at the time of your order.


All of our instruments are play-tested and set up before sale. If an item could not be function-tested, this is stated in the description. All photos are of the actual item, so please refer to them to get an idea of its condition.


As this is a used item, it may show scratches or wear. We cannot accept returns for dents, dings, scratches, or cosmetic wear that are already shown in the photos or noted in the description. Returns are accepted only for defects not mentioned in the description, and only within one week of delivery. If damage during shipping is suspected, confirmation with the carrier and an insurance claim will be required, so please be sure to keep the packaging materials and the item in the condition they arrived in (see our policy).


[Voltage Notice]

The voltage in Japan is 100V, which differs from that of most other countries.

Please check the voltage in your country before purchasing — if it differs, you may need a voltage converter/adapter to use the item in your country.


[Customs Duties]

Depending on your country and the item price, customs duties may be charged in addition to the item price and shipping cost.


We kindly ask that you check the import duties and fees you may incur before purchasing.


* Italy in particular is highly likely to charge additional customs fees, so please be sure to check before purchasing.

Cosmetically, it is very clean with only minimal signs of use, if any. It has been gigged once and has spent most of its time stored safely when not in use.

A great opportunity to get a nearly new Player Plus Precision Bass without paying full retail.

item.title: 1988 Erine Ball Music Man Stingray Bass 1988 - Translucent Red - 4-Bolt, 2-Band EQ (with Extras!) — $3,450
item.description:
$3,450 · Very Good · Not Bob's Shop

Up for sale is a beautiful 1988 Ernie Ball Music Man StingRay 4-String Bass. The neck pocket is factory-stamped "REDT Jan 7 1988," confirming production date and factory desirable Translucent Red finish. Body is single piece Ash with nice grain and full maple neck has the nice figuring typical of this period.

This bass features the legendary combination that players and vintage collectors hunt for: the early 4-bolt neck plate and the classic 3-knob, 2-band EQ (Volume, Treble, Bass) configuration. This specific era delivers that iconic, raw, punchy growl and slap tone that made the StingRay famous.

Electronics & Originality:

  • Preamp Upgrade: The electronics have been upgraded to a premium, period-correct Mojotone MM Clone V2. This boutique board is a 1:1 exact component replica of Leo Fender's classic 2-band circuit. It is built with custom-tapered vintage pots and the rare, original LM4250CN IC chip for 100% authentic vintage tone with modern reliability.
  • Originality: the original factory stock circuit board was safely preserved and is included in the sale. This allows the next owner to enjoy flawless, noise-free performance today while retaining 100% of the instrument's long-term vintage collector value.
  • Pickup: The original late-1980s factory humbucker pickup is untouched and original to the bass.

Condition Note:

  • Neck & Frets: Absolutely excellent condition. The neck is straight, and the frets have plenty of life with no dead spots, deep divots, or buzzing. It offers low, fast action.
  • Body: Shows honest, vintage character from nearly 40 years of love. There is buckle rash on the back that has not broken through the translucent red finish, along with typical small surface dings and about a half-dozen small paint chips along the edges.
  • Structure: No deep wood gouges, extra drilled holes, cracks, or structural issues whatsoever. A rock-solid player's vintage machine.

Case: Ships safely in a premium, period-correct vintage black Tolex hardwood hardshell case with a striking blue velour interior that complements the translucent red finish beautifully. (Note: This is a premium aftermarket case, not the factory Music Man branded case).

Shipping & Setup:The bass will be professionally packed with the strings slacked and the headstock securely supported to prevent any movement in transit. Fully insured shipping via Reverb Safe Shipping.

Flat-rate shipping is $120 to cover a heavy-duty bass box, professional packing materials, and full Reverb Safe Shipping insurance. If you live regionally and the actual shipping label cost ends up being significantly less, I will gladly refund you the exact cash difference immediately after printing the label.

item.title: Burns London Scorpion Bass - Satin Black — $799
item.description:
$799 · Very Good · Hazelwood Acres Musical

Very good condition, electronics are clean & function as they should. Looks to be all original aside from the added black speed knobs, & the strap button relocations from the previous owner. No case included.

From England to the world. Jim Burns created the Burns company in the 60's as a true alternative to American instruments. Instead of just making lesser copies of famous US guitars, Burns had his own ideas about how a guitar should look, perform and sound.

The highly acclaimed band The Shadows used Burns instruments exclusively for years, helping spread the name of these instruments.

The Burns guitar company has gone through all types of stages, and have closed and reopened several times.

SCORPION BASS

The Scorpion Bass might look out of place next to the other reissue Burns Basses (Bison and Marquee models), but this is also in fact a recreation of a Burns bass from yesteryear. Ths original Scorpion Bass was launched in 1979 and clearly shows that Burns were aiming at a younger and "angrier" type of bass player. Heavy Metal was born around these days, and the Scorpion bass fitted right in.

To obtain the maximum punch, Burns chose a standar 34" scale for this bass.

Electronics are maybe more straightforward than other Burns models, with the tried and tested P/J pickup arrangement, controlled by 2 Volume, 1 Tone, and 1 three way Telecaster type lever switch. Of course there is a hidden plus: a miniswitch to connect both pickups out of phase, producing a thin and nasal effect, perfect for guitar-like parts and as an effect too for intros, etc.

item.title: Wingbass NT-5 - Satin Finish (2024, like new! Nano POG onboard!) — $1,500
item.description:
$1,500 · Excellent · Vince on Reverb

You have found a Wingbass NT-5 in brand new condition, with gig bag.
The WingBass NT-5 is the same in all aspects as a standard 34" scale bass guitar, but with the first register (octave) of the neck not present. It has exclusively the second octave. Outstanding to work on upper register skills, for travel, and very easy playing at home.

Included is the original Wing Instruments strap, and an extra strap. The black tape-wound strings currently on there are from the manufacturer (Wing Instruments), and they are not fully broken in. I bought this from the original owner, who had bought it directly from the manufacturer. Neither of us played it much. I hardly played it at all because of relocation and job commitments. I lament this, because this is truly a beautiful instrument with top-notch craftsmanship.

• Built-in Nano POG. This particular bass has the Nano POG octave pedal electronics installed in the instrument, so your pedalboard does not need an octave pedal. The sound is amazing!!!
• Black Limba Body.
• Bartolini Pickup.
• Mohagany Neck Through.

When things settle for me, I would love to have a 6-string Wing Bass NT-6.

Priced to sell!! Immediate shipment!!!
$1,500. Free shipping to continental USA.
Continental USA sale only.

Wing Instruments Website: WingInstruments dot com

item.title: Warwick Streamer $$ Fretless 2006 - Honey Violin — $1,910.64
item.description:
$1,910.64 · Very Good · Stathan's Bass Shop

Up for sale is a magnificent Made in Germany Warwick Streamer $$ (Double Buck) Fretless 4-string, in excellent condition. This is a factory-original fretless instrument, not a conversion, featuring an unlined boutique Ebony fingerboard that delivers an incredible, singing "mwah" tone with endless sustain.


To my knowledge, the Streamer was only released as a $$ (Double Buck) model for a single year, and a fretless version was never 'officially' cataloged, making this a highly rare instrument.


This bass is a tone monster, perfectly suited for everything from progressive metal (think Beyond Creation/Obscura style tech-death) to jazz-fusion and fluid session work. The bridge saddles and nut have been upgraded to bone for a more even sound and endless mwah.


Condition: The bass is in excellent structural and cosmetic shape, showing only minor, normal signs of use which are clearly shown in the pictures. The ebony fingerboard is clean and in great condition. Electronics are fully functional, dead quiet, and the truss rod works perfectly.


Shipping: Will be packed with professional care and shipped fully insured across Europe. Please contact me for accurate shipping rates to your specific location or if you have any questions.

item.title: Sire Marcus Miller P5 4-String Bass — $475
item.description:

This p-bass was originally purchased in March of 2024.  It has served as a secondary instrument to my other basses for when I wanted to play a more traditional p-bass.  The bass has been played at home and has not been gigged. I acquired another p-bass earlier this year and have decided to let this one go.

There are a few light marks near the strap pins from some strap rub (tried to get this in the photos).  Also, there are some light scratches in the pickguard below and to the right of the pickup.These occurred when using my fingernails to try to peel the darn “Ready to Play” sticker that Sire stuck on the pickguard (see original photo from Sweetwater).  The protective plastic had already been peeled off, and they plopped a sticker on the pickguard (really Sire?).  It took some work to get the sticker off, and unfortunately the pickguard scratched easily.  So, unfortunately, those scratches have been there since day one but luckily are not very noticeable unless one looks closely.

item.title: Gibson EB-2 1964 - 1972 - Sunburst — $2,970.75
item.description:
$2,970.75 · Good · JJ’s Joints

A lovely mid 60’s Gibson EB-2 up for sale. This one embodies what every vintage instrument should look like after surviving 59 years of abuse. It certainly is a shame that most of these cool old guitars find themselves in the closet of a wealthy businessman’s closet and not touring the world for people to enjoy.

Fortunately, that is not the case with this one. It has toured coast to coast several times over and been thoroughly loved. Despite its wear, this guitar is heck of a player and the original pickup sounds delightful. The neck is quite chunky and feels much more like an earlier 60’s Gibson bass. It has been refretted and was given a clean bill of health by MacKinnon Guitar Co. Apart from the missing mute and pickguard, the bass is all original and will be shipped in it’s original case.
